question: You want a single ticket for a train. Which short phrase asks for that?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|Un billete sencillo, por favor.||UN BIYE-te SEN-seeyo, POR fa-BOR.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|Una mesa para dos, por favor.||U-na ME-sa PA-ra DOS, POR fa-BOR.}}
{{es-ES|La cuenta, por favor.||LA CUEN-ta, POR fa-BOR.}}
{{es-ES|¿Dónde está el baño?||DON-de ES-ta EL BA-no?}}
explanation: "Billete" (or "boleto" in many regions) with "sencillo" signals a one-way or single ticket in travel contexts.

question: In Spanish, at the airport, which word usually means "passport"?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|pasaporte||pa-sa-POR-te}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|maleta||ma-LE-ta}}
{{es-ES|vuelo||BUE-lo}}
{{es-ES|puerta||PUER-ta}}
explanation: "Pasaporte" is the standard word for passport.

question: You need the main train station. Which word often means "station" in this context?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|estación||es-TA-seeon}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|calle||CAYE}}
{{es-ES|plaza||PLA-za}}
{{es-ES|hotel||ho-TEL}}
explanation: "Estación" is the common word for a station, including train stations.

question: In Spanish, which phrase politely asks where the nearest bank is?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Dónde está el banco más cercano?||DON-de ES-ta EL BAN-co MAS sehr-CA-no?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿Cuánto cuesta?||CUAN-to CUES-ta?}}
{{es-ES|¿Tiene menú en inglés?||TIE-ne ME-nu EN IN-gles?}}
{{es-ES|Quisiera el menú del día.||qui-SIE-ra EL ME-nu DEL DIA.}}
explanation: "¿Dónde está...?" asks where something is; "banco" means bank and "cercano" means nearby.

question: In Spanish, which word means "left" when you give or follow directions?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|izquierda||iz-KIER-da}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|derecha||de-RE-cha}}
{{es-ES|recto||REC-to}}
{{es-ES|cerca||SEHR-ca}}
explanation: "Izquierda" means left; "a la izquierda" is a common direction phrase.

question: At a hotel front desk, which phrase asks if they have a free room?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Tienen una habitación libre?||TIE-nen U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon LI-bre?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿A qué hora sale el vuelo?||A KE HO-ra SA-le EL BUE-lo?}}
{{es-ES|Un café con leche, por favor.||UN CA-fe CON LE-che, POR fa-BOR.}}
{{es-ES|¿Cuánto cuesta la entrada?||CUAN-to CUES-ta LA en-TRA-da?}}
explanation: "Habitación" means room; "libre" or "disponible" often signals availability.

question: How do you ask 'Where is the bathroom?' in Spanish?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Dónde está el baño?||DON-de ES-ta EL BA-no?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿Cuándo está el baño?||CUAN-do ES-ta EL BA-no?}}
{{es-ES|¿Qué es el baño?||QUE ES EL BA-no?}}
{{es-ES|¿Cómo está el baño?||CO-mo ES-ta EL BA-no?}}
explanation: '¿Dónde está?' = 'Where is?'. Replace 'el baño' with any place: la estación, el hotel, etc.

question: How do you ask 'How much does this cost?' in Spanish?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Cuánto cuesta esto?||CUAN-to CUES-ta ES-to?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿Qué es esto?||QUE ES ES-to?}}
{{es-ES|¿Dónde compro esto?||DON-de COM-pro ES-to?}}
{{es-ES|¿Hay descuento?||HAY des-CUEN-to?}}
explanation: '¿Cuánto cuesta?' — 'How much does it cost?' — the key phrase for any market or shop.

question: In beginner Spanish, how do you say 'I have a reservation.'?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|Tengo una reserva.||TEN-go U-na re-SER-ba.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿Tiene una habitación individual?||TIE-ne U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon in-di-bi-DUAL?}}
{{es-ES|¿Tiene una habitación doble?||TIE-ne U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon DO-ble?}}
{{es-ES|La contraseña del wifi, por favor.||LA con-tra-SE-na DEL WI-fi, POR fa-BOR.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: Which Spanish phrase means 'Do you have a single room?'?
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Tiene una habitación individual?||TIE-ne U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon in-di-bi-DUAL?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|¿Tiene una habitación doble?||TIE-ne U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon DO-ble?}}
{{es-ES|La contraseña del wifi, por favor.||LA con-tra-SE-na DEL WI-fi, POR fa-BOR.}}
{{es-ES|Hay un problema con la habitación.||HAY UN pro-BLE-ma CON LA ha-bi-TA-seeon.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: Choose the correct Spanish phrase for 'Do you have a double room?'.
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|¿Tiene una habitación doble?||TIE-ne U-na ha-bi-TA-seeon DO-ble?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{es-ES|La contraseña del wifi, por favor.||LA con-tra-SE-na DEL WI-fi, POR fa-BOR.}}

{{es-ES|La ducha no funciona.||LA DU-cha NO fun-SEEO-na.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: In beginner Spanish, how do you say 'There is a problem with the room.'?

correctAnswer: {{es-ES|La ducha no funciona.||LA DU-cha NO fun-SEEO-na.}}

question: Choose the correct Spanish phrase for 'The room is too cold.'.
correctAnswer: {{es-ES|La habitación está demasiado fría.||LA ha-bi-TA-seeon ES-ta de-ma-SIA-do FRIA.}}
